source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
A 3-bus power system is shown in the <image>, where the diagonal elements of Y-bus matrix are \( Y_{11} = -j12 \text{pu} \), \( Y_{22} = -j15 \text{pu} \), and \( Y_{33} = -j7 \text{pu} \).

The per unit values of the line reactances \( p \), \( q \), and \( r \) shown in the figure are:
extraction_notes: image field contains an in-memory PIL image placeholder, so no image path could be extracted
raw_answer_text was mapped from option letter B to its corresponding choice text

issues: metadata.extraction_notes states that no image path could be extracted, which is inconsistent with the provided image path and fallback image manifest.
metadata.image_paths is empty while top-level image_paths contains a provided image path.

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says 'shown in the figure'
multi_step: the task asks for three unknown line reactances p, q, r rather than a single direct lookup
question combines numerical Y-bus diagonal entries with figure-based network structure
solving typically requires mapping bus connectivity from the figure and relating diagonal Y-bus terms to incident line admittances
verifiability: answer is a specific numeric triplet (p, q, r)
multiple-choice options are provided, enabling straightforward answer checking
the result can be checked against the given Y-bus diagonal values using standard power-system relations

risk_flags: asset registry reports metadata/image-path inconsistencies in issues
image content itself is not available in the input payload, so topology-specific reasoning cannot be directly confirmed here

decision_reasons: Core assets are present: question text, answer, and one existing image are all available.
Initial scoring is strong, with high image dependency (0.96), good multi-step complexity (0.7), and high verifiability (0.9).
The sample appears recoverable and useful because the figure-dependent question still has its supporting image asset recorded in the manifest.
Reported issues are metadata inconsistencies about image paths, but they do not negate the presence of the actual image file or the core semantic assets.
